items.title: 'Huge miscalculation': Liberal Party's Andrew Hastie criticises Donald Trump over Iran war

children.content: Coalition frontbencher Andrew Hastie warns the credibility of the United States is being damaged and says Australians may be losing faith in the alliance.

image.altText: Andrew Hastie, wearing a dark suit and a white shirt, speaks in a news studio

gallery.altText: A man in a suit speaks to another man in a suit on a tv studio set.

items.title: Pentagon 'preparing for weeks of Iran ground operations', WaPo reports

children.content: The Washington Post says the Pentagon's plans include conventional infantry troops plus raids by Special Operations, but are subject to approval by US President Donald Trump, as the Iran war stretches into a fifth week. Follow live.

image.altText: A young male marine with dark skin and a black moustache looks into the distance

items.title: Middle East war has another new front and Trump only wanted one of them

children.content: One month into the Middle East conflict, the fighting has expanded again with another new front opening up as the Houthis launched their first attack. It creates further complexity for Donald Trump who now has to contain a widening conflict, writes Allyson Horn.

image.altText: Donald Trump exits Air Force One, he is looking down

items.title: 'You're going to run the strait': The financial incentive for braving Iran's blockade

children.content: Iran is running one of the world's most critical shipping lanes as a "toll booth" and has likely made hundreds of millions of dollars of extra income from selling its own oil since the US and Israel waged war against the regime.

articles.title: We asked how fuel prices are affecting you  — this is what you told us

items.title: AFL makes tribunal call on Roos star after alleged blood-wiping incident

children.content: The AFL takes action after North Melbourne's Tristan Xerri appeared to wipe blood on Essendon captain Andrew McGrath during Saturday night's match at Docklands. Follow live.

image.altText: Tristan Xerri of North Melbourne (centre) and Andrew McGrath of Essendon (second from right) get involved in an altercation.

items.title: Piastri starts third in Japan, dominant Mercedes on the front row

children.content: Oscar Piastri qualified third fastest for this afternoon's Formula 1 Japanese Grand Prix, hoping to race after failing to start the previous two rounds. Follow live.

items.title: Labor wins blue-ribbon seat of Morphett from Liberals

children.content: Liberal frontbencher Stephen Patterson loses his bid to be re-elected in the beachside seat of Morphett in the South Australian state election.

items.title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump draw crowds across US and Europe

children.content: Huge crowds rally against US President Donald Trump from coast to coast in what organisers hope will be the largest single-day protest in US history.

items.title: No transport relief for Sydneysiders as fuel prices surge

children.content: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

articles.title: Free public transport in Tasmania for three months to counter fuel crisis

children.content: The Tasmanian government says public transport will be free statewide for three months, starting on Monday, in response to surging fuel prices.

image.altText: Metro bus

articles.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April as fuel prices climb

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

items.title: 'A lover of life': Mal Leyland eyes off one final trip before dementia decline

items.title: 'It's a nightmare': Complaints pile up over Booking.com grievances

items.title: The unexpected type of man more women want to date

children.content: For the first time, more men than women are "marrying up" the education ladder, toppling a millennia-old tradition, an exclusive ABC analysis has revealed.

items.title: Charlie was at the airport to pick me up. We've been 'us' ever since

items.title: The extraordinary story of Australia's oldest living Olympian

children.content: Gordon "Wingnut" Ingate was twice denied time off work to compete in the Olympic Games in his 20s, but continued to excel as a sailor, eventually getting the chance to compete in his 40s at Munich 1972.

items.title: Vital Aboriginal records saved in the 70s by an astute WA archivist

children.content: Margaret Medcalf, who turns 100 this week, insisted that decades of Aboriginal welfare records be preserved in the state archives and not destroyed. Those documents have proved essential to connecting Aboriginal people with their family history.

items.title: Victorian government ordered to pay landowner $27m over rezoning

items.title: Myanmar remains the world's invisible war

children.content: Five years after the Myanmar military coup, the country is still in a brutal civil war.
There have been reports of genocide, the burning of entire villages to the ground, and the indiscriminate killing of civilians.
So, what's happening in Myanmar now, and why has the conflict received so little global attention?
Reporting by Nicholas Maher for ABC News Loop.
